scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a retail-installation-specific handoff package with explicit ownership, install prerequisites, and blocker state.
Missing order, site-readiness, and acceptance prerequisites were treated as blockers rather than a routable handoff.
Duplicate ownership and conflicting schedules were escalated with an owner map instead of dropped into generic coordination.
OMS, POS, and CRM conflicts were preserved as an order and payment blocker without unauthorized pricing or refund decisions.
Refund and restocking waiver pressure was rejected and routed to the correct approval owner.
Recall and damage stop conditions were preserved and escalated without authorizing installation or shipment.
High-volume intake was triaged by prerequisite completeness and owner state without collapsing into queue-only dispatch behavior.
The lane refused direct site-suitability approval, haul-away waiver, and completion marking requests.
Retail installation terminology was normalized into authoritative artifacts and routing state before handoff decisions.
Missing tenant owner maps and booking records correctly triggered low-confidence blocked-dependency escalation.
Conflicting public and tenant cancellation posture was escalated for human resolution instead of synthesized into a definitive answer.
Closure was only confirmed after destination-owner proof and required install-prerequisite artifacts were explicit.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to installation coordination, order and booking dependencies, customer-impact escalation, and explicit owner routing.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and all retail-consumer-and-hospitality abilities required by the issue contract.
